title: Two-phase validate-then-apply is the right RFC 8620 §7.1 atomicity architecture
description: Phase 1 validates all member patch paths and values before Phase 2 touches the store. This is the correct architecture for input-error atomicity: a combined {name + bad members/* role} patch must not write the name even though name is individually valid. A future contributor might suggest 'just validate inline in apply_member_patch and return early' — that would re-introduce the atomicity violation. The two-pass approach is load-bearing.
